steps.title: Set up mass and silicon balance equations
steps.content:
Let $m_1$ = mass of the first piece (30% silicon) and $m_2$ = mass of the second piece (80% silicon).

The total mass equation is:
$$m_1 + m_2 = 50.0$$
For silicon content, the final sample is 50% silicon by mass, so it contains $0.50 \times 50.0 = 25.0$ grams of silicon. The silicon balance equation is:
$$0.30m_1 + 0.80m_2 = 25.0$$

steps.title: Solve for the mass of the second piece
Calculate silicon mass in the second piece
steps.content:
The mass of silicon in the second piece is 80% of its total mass:
$$0.80 \times 20.0 = 16.0 \text{ grams}$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
